behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module handoff (request builder -> API client). A unit test on only a formatter can miss dropped labels during wiring/serialization. No real OS/network/subprocess is required (System rejected), and correctness does not depend on model judgment (Agentic rejected). REJECTION-GATE: rejected System/Agentic because behavior text is about request contents, not live infra effects or model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Wire classification module with a stub API transport and assert the outbound payload contains the full taxonomy list for multiple taxonomy sizes/orders; fail if any label is missing at dispatch boundary.

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns "unknown" instead of forwarding the unexpected value to the caller.
behaviors.justification: This is local normalization/validation logic on one response value. No cross-module failure is required, no real infra needed, and no dependence on model quality.
behaviors.test_description: Call the normalization/decision function with out-of-taxonomy labels (case/whitespace variants included) and assert output is exactly "unknown".

behaviors.description: For identical raw API responses, the module's normalization step always produces the same final label.
behaviors.justification: Pure determinism/idempotence of normalization for identical input; single local function/object boundary. Higher levels add no unique failure signal.
behaviors.test_description: Run normalization repeatedly on byte-identical raw responses and assert identical final label each run.

behaviors.description: On repeated live API calls for the same document and taxonomy, the returned label remains consistent unless the provider response itself changes.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires repeated live AI API calls and consistency tied to provider output; mocking would hide real model/provider variability. Not Workflow because this is repeated single-step classification, not a multi-stage agent pipeline. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Workflow because behavior text describes one decision step repeated, not chained agentic stages.
behaviors.test_description: Execute N live calls for fixed document+taxonomy, record provider raw response and final label, and assert label drift only occurs when provider response content changes.

behaviors.description: Request construction enforces the configured token budget before dispatch (for example truncate/reject according to policy).
behaviors.justification: Failure appears at boundary between token-budget policy/config and dispatch path (must prove enforcement occurs before send). In-process wiring with stub transport is sufficient. System rejected: no real OS/network needed. Agentic rejected: no dependence on model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Configure strict budget, invoke classify with oversized input, and assert module applies configured truncate/reject policy and does not dispatch over-budget payload to transport stub.

behaviors.description: Provider-reported token usage for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: This behavior depends on real AI provider execution and provider-reported usage fields; a mock can trivially pass and hide real over-budget behavior. By LLM-DEP rule, path through real AI/ML API call with correctness tied to returned usage stays Agentic. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration because behavior text requires provider-reported usage, which stubs can mask.
behaviors.test_description: Run live classification on representative documents, capture provider usage metadata, and assert reported total tokens are <= configured budget threshold.

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: This is retry orchestration across module boundaries (error mapping, backoff/wait, retry dispatch). Can be caught with in-process fake API + fake clock; real network/subprocess not required. Agentic rejected because assertions are on retry control flow, not model decision quality.
behaviors.test_description: Use a stub provider that returns rate-limit signal then success; assert module waits per backoff policy, retries, and returns successful label instead of bubbling initial error.
